company_type: Technology company in the global payments industry
segments: ACH Batch and Real-time Account-based Payments

dependencies: Reliance on third-party service providers for timely transmission of information across global data network
Dependence on issuing and acquiring customers for business success
Dependence on technology infrastructure and systems
Reliance on third-party providers for cybersecurity and operational resilience
risks: Service disruptions from third-party service providers could interrupt services
Supply-chain attacks could cause system interruptions
Inadequate infrastructure in lesser-developed markets could result in service disruptions
Third-party provider failures could impact operations
Consolidation among customers could result in substantial loss of business
Customer financial instability could lead to settlement risk exposure

regulatory_risks: Central banks and regulators establishing formal oversight over payments industry participants
Systemic importance regulation in the European Union with governance and risk management requirements
Bank of England designation of Vocalink as 'specified service provider' with supervision requirements
Mastercard designated as 'recognized payment system' in UK with examination requirements
Regulation as payments institutions and payment service providers including money transmitters
Interchange fee regulation and caps (EU legislation capping consumer credit and debit interchange fees)
U.S. legislation capping debit interchange for certain regulated activities
Proposed U.S. legislation to extend routing mandates to credit cards
Illinois Interchange Fee Prohibition Act prohibiting interchange on tax and gratuity portions
Merchant surcharging regulation and no-surcharge rule limitations
Data localization requirements in India, China, Saudi Arabia and other jurisdictions
Anti-money laundering (AML) and countering financing of terrorism (CFT) laws globally including Bank Secrecy Act and USA PATRIOT Act
Economic sanctions programs administered by U.S. OFAC and European Union
Anti-corruption laws including U.S. Foreign Corrupt Practices Act and U.K. Bribery Act
Payment Services Directive in EEA requiring third-party payment processor access
Strong authentication requirements in EU potentially increasing transaction abandonment
Privacy and data protection laws including GDPR in EU and state data privacy laws in U.S.
AI regulation including EU AI Act
SEC disclosure rules requiring cybersecurity incident reporting within four business days
GLBA information safeguarding requirements
Open banking regulations including U.S. CFPB rule on data sharing
Retail Payment Activities Act registration requirements in Canada
Environmental, social and governance disclosure requirements including EU CSRD and potential SEC climate rules
Antitrust and competition law investigations and proceedings
Regulation of internet transactions and high-risk merchant categories
OECD Pillar 2 Rules implementing 15% global minimum tax
Tax law changes and regulatory guidance affecting multinational enterprises
Preferential treatment or protection for domestic payment providers by governments
Foreign ownership restrictions potentially forcing technology transfer
Requirements to obtain prior approval for system rules, procedures or operations changes

high_risk_countries: Ukraine (Crimea, Donetsk People's Republic, Luhansk People's Republic regions)
sanction_risks: OFAC sanctions restricting transactions with certain countries and regions (Crimea, Donetsk, Luhansk, Cuba, Iran, North Korea, Syria)
Compliance with OFAC Specially Designated Nationals and Blocked Persons (SDN List)
Russia sanctions and suspension of business operations in Russia
Potential for affected jurisdictions to take retaliatory actions
Risk of nationalization of subsidiary in Russia
Iran and Syria identified as terrorist-sponsoring states
Geopolitical events leading to information security threats and attacks
Potential for jurisdictions to grow local payment networks in response to sanctions
U.S.-China trade tensions and potential impacts
Risk of fines, lawsuits, and operational challenges from sanctions compliance
